original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: United States Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Post-Travel Evaluation of the Ill Traveler
original_registry_entry.version: CDC Yellow Book: Health Information for International Travel, 2026 edition
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: United States federal public-health guidance for international travel; requires UAE communicable-disease and referral adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Ill recent international travelers, with destination, itinerary, timing, exposure, host, syndrome and incubation-period qualifiers.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Initial clinical triage and evaluation after international travel.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for travel history, exposure, timing, syndrome and initial evaluation documentation. It does not generate a diagnosis, test panel, isolation decision, treatment, referral or urgency classification.

exact_sections.heading: Introduction and triage of the ill returned traveler
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording recent international travel, illness timing, severity, vital-sign and infection-control context, with urgent decisions left to the assessing clinician.

exact_sections.heading: The post-travel evaluation — association with travel and exposures
exact_sections.locator: official chapter sections “Association of the traveler’s illness with (recent) travel” and “Exposures”
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports itinerary, subnational destination, dates, travel style, accommodation, bites, animals, freshwater, food, water, sexual and fellow-traveler illness history without assigning an infection.

exact_sections.heading: Clinical evaluation — host factors, syndromic approach and incubation context
exact_sections.locator: official chapter clinical-evaluation and incubation-period sections
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician recording of underlying conditions, medicines, vaccination, prophylaxis, symptom syndrome, onset relative to travel and incubation context without generating tests or a diagnosis.

title: Post-Travel Evaluation of the Ill Traveler
publisher: United States Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
jurisdiction: United States federal public-health guidance for international travel; requires UAE communicable-disease and referral adaptation
population: Ill recent international travelers, with destination, itinerary, timing, exposure, host, syndrome and incubation-period qualifiers.
intended_setting: Initial clinical triage and evaluation after international travel.
